title: LLM Skills Runner Code Walkthrough
description: Explore the architecture and execution flow of the LLM Skills Runner toolkit that enables LLMs to execute skills with Python code.

steps.subtitle: This file serves as the entry point for the CLI application. It imports and runs the main CLI function, making the skills runner executable from the command line.

The CLI chat command initializes the configuration from environment variables, creates an LLM client and conversation instance, then either processes a single prompt or enters interactive mode. It demonstrates the core workflow of the skills runner.

The Conversation class manages chat history and tool execution. It initializes with an LLM client, tools list, and skills folder path, setting up a system message that guides the LLM to use the list_skills tool first.

This method runs a conversation turn by sending messages to the LLM, processing tool calls if present, executing them via _execute_tool, and appending results back to the conversation history. It loops until a final assistant response is returned.

This method dispatches tool calls to their respective handlers. It supports list_skills, get_skill, read_files_in_skill, and run_python_script tools by delegating to skill-specific functions in skills_tool.py. It handles JSON parsing errors and unknown tools gracefully.

The LLMClient class handles communication with OpenAI-compatible LLM APIs. It sends chat completion requests with messages and tools, processes the response, and raises errors for API failures or invalid responses.

This constant defines the list of available tools that can be called by the LLM. It includes four core tools: list_skills, get_skill, read_files_in_skill, and run_python_script that enable skill discovery and execution.

The list_skills function scans the configured skills folder and returns a list of valid skill directories. It validates skill names to prevent directory traversal attacks and logs execution duration for performance monitoring.

This function safely reads files within a skill's directory by validating the file path to prevent directory traversal. It returns file content, size, and encoding information while handling various error conditions.

The run_python_script function executes Python code in a skill's virtual environment. It finds the appropriate Python executable, runs the script with timeout protection, and returns stdout/stderr results or error information.
